State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 9581.

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Inte
(916) 322-5662                322-5660              322-5901              322-4441              322-4444

February 11, 1982

Sally Kipper
Friends of Lou Papan
P. O. Box 892
Daly City, CA 94017

Dear Sally:

I have reviewed copies of Schedule E for the Friends of Lou
Papan committee which you sent to me on February 3, 1982.

In response to your inquires as to how to report vendors paid
by charge cards, the amounts shown on Schedule E should correspond to the actual amount of a particular check written to
a specific payee (i.e. Mastercharge, Visa or American Express).
If the payee is receiving reimbursed amounts, as you often
receive from the committee, or if it is a payment for a service
charged to a charge card, then the actual payee should be listed
with the full amount of the payment and any individual reimbursed or charged amount of $100 or more to a single payee
should be listed below the actual payee as person who have
provided consideration for an expenditure of $100 or more.
(See Gov. Code Section 84211(i) and (j)(6)). For example:

American Express Co                                    $575
P. O. Box 13764              Misc. Expenses
Phoenix, Ariz. 35002

Vendors of $100 or more:

Frank Fats                    $167
L St.
Sacramento, CA

Max's Son Restaurant $109
Serramonte Plaza
Daly City, CA

Sally Kipper                  $399
43 Surrey               Reimbursed for Misc.
San Francisco, CA            Expenses

Sally Kipper
Friends of Lou Papan

-2-

Vendors of $100 or more:

ABC Office Supply          $260
123 Union St.
San Francisco, CA

I hope that the previous examples clarify the proper method for
reporting expenditures by the Friends of Lou Papan committee.
If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact
me.

                                        Yours Very Truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        Lynn Montgomery
                                        Political Reform Consultant

LM/cm

Friends of Lori Poppin

P.O. BOX 992, DALY CITY, CA 94017

February 5, 1981

Fair Political Practices Com.
Technical Assistance Division
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

ATTN: Lynn Montgomery

Dear Lynn:

I'm sending you copies of Schedules E and G from our last filing
to ask for your comment and/or suggestion on several points:

1. American Express, Mastercard and Visa entries (pp. 1,3 & 5
respectively) include only the charges under $100. Charges over
$100: instead of listing them as vendors under the cards, I've
listed them alphabetically, indicating which card they were
paid by. It seems this is a more accurate picture because the
same vendor often shows up on several charge cards (ex: Max's
Son Restaurant, p. 5) and this shows the total to one vendor all
in one place. Nevertheless, we'll be glad to do it differently
if FPPC prefers.

2. Payment to Frank Fat's, p. 2(see also copies of invoices, p.6):
The amount paid includes two separate checks, one of which was
over $100. I felt the two could be lumped together because no
individual charge on either invoice exceeded $100.

3. The same holds for reimbursements to me (last entry, p.2).
The one check to me for an individual amount of $100 is listed
separately.

4. Frequently there are several entries for one vendor (ex: Max's
Sons, p.5) that all have the same description of expenditure.
Under that circumstance, what is the advantage to the public from
a disclosure standpoint of listing them separately? A better
example than Max's might be several checks to the same stationer,
all for office supplies, and all of which are written directly
to the vendor rather than via a charge card.

5. I had asked you earlier to clarify a procedure for showing
activity from our federal committee on our state campaign state-
ment (per David Cook's suggestion). I did that on Schedule E,
see p.5, and on Schedule G, see p.7. What do you think?

Fair Political Practices Com.
Lynn Montgomery
Page 2

February 5, 1981

I'd appreciate knowing if there is a problem with any of these
things so that we can correct them on future reports. Thanks for
your assistance.

Sincerely,

Sally Kipper

Enclosure

embedding.first_500_words: February 11, 1982 Sally Kipper Friends of Lou Papan P.O. Box 892 Daly City, CA 94017 Dear Sally: I have reviewed copies of Schedule E for the Friends of Lou Papan committee which you sent to me on February 3, 1982. In response to your inquiries as to how to report vendors paid by charge cards, the amounts shown on Schedule E should correspond to the actual amount of a particular check written to a specific payee (i.e. Mastercharge, Visa or American Express). If the payee is receiving reimbursed amounts, as you often receive from the committee, or if it is a payment for a service charged to a charge card, then the actual payee should be listed with the full amount of the payment and any individual reimbursed or charged amount of $100 or more to a single payee should be listed below the actual payee as person who have provided consideration for an expenditure of $100 or more. (See Gov. Code Section 84211(i) and (j)(6)). For example: American Express Co P.O. Box 13764 Phoenix, Ariz. 85002 Misc. Expenses $576 Vendors of $100 or more: Frank Fats $167 L St. Sacramento, CA Max's Son Restaurant $109 Serramonte Plaza Daly City, CA Sally Kipper $539 48 Surrey San Francisco, CA Reimbursed for Misc. Expenses Sally Kipper Friends of Lou Papan Vendors of $100 or more: ABC Office Supply $260 123 Union St. San Francisco, CA I hope that the previous examples clarify the proper method for reporting expenditures by the Friends of Lou Papan committee. If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact me. Yours Very Truly, [Signature] Lynn Montgomery Political Reform Consultant LM/cm February 3, 1981 Fair Political Practices Com. Technical Assistance Division 1100 K Street Sacramento, CA 95814 ATTN: Lynn Montgomery Dear Lynn: I'm sending you copies of Schedules E and G from our last filing to ask for your comment and/or suggestion on several points: 1. American Express, Mastercard and Visa entries (pp. 1, 3 & 5 respectively) include only the charges under $100. Charges over $100: instead of listing them as vendors under the cards, I've listed them alphabetically, indicating which card they were paid by. It seems this is a more accurate picture because the same vendor often shows up on several charge cards (ex: Max's Son Restaurant, p. 3) and this shows the total to one vendor all in one place. Nevertheless, we'll be glad to do it differently if FPPC prefers. 2. Payment to Frank Fat's, p. 2 (see also copies of invoices, p.6): The Amount paid includes two separate checks, one of which was over $100. I felt the two could be lumped together because no individual charge on either invoice exceeded $100. 3. The same holds for reimbursements to me (last entry, p.2). The one check to me for an individual amount of $100 is listed separately. 4. Frequently there are several entries for one vendor (ex: Max's Sons, p.3) that all have the same description of expenditure.
